pending_context.whisper: "Wall-Hand Louisiana" (highway-21 traffic-stop episode where Cynthia was arrested for not saying "yes sir")
pending_context.candidate_correction: (LA town on Highway 21 between Covington and Bogalusa — likely Folsom or Sun, LA)
pending_context.notes: Cynthia describes her traffic stop: "I stopped behind a school bus. It wouldn't used to have a railroad track when you just you got on 21. It was a state trooper behind me. ... He pulled me over ... They put me in jail. My bond was $4.00." Hwy 21 between Covington and Bogalusa passes through Sun and Folsom LA; "Wall-Hand" is an unidentified Whisper rendering. Adversarial flag — possibly Walnut LA, possibly an unincorporated locality.

pending_context.whisper: "Andrew Moses" (resigning Bogalusa Civic and Voters League president, early 1965)
pending_context.notes: Pass 1's row 25.9 conflated Andrew Moses with A.Z. Young; this row separates the figure out. Interviewer Mosnier introduces him as a real predecessor figure: "younger folks, including Easy Young and Robert Hicks and Gail Jenkins, effectively take control of the vote of the voters. Andrew Moses has resigned." Adversarial cross-check needed against Bogalusa Civic and Voters League roster pre-1965.

pending_context.whisper: "Michael Richmond" (one of two first-elected Black city councilmen, alongside William Bayley/Bailey)
pending_context.notes: "And we got out for a city elected official, which was William Bayley and Michael Richmond." First-person attestation of two of the first Black Bogalusa city councilmen. Speaker-originating — adversarial cross-check against Bogalusa city council roster late 1960s/early 1970s would canonicalize.

pending_context.whisper: "Milton Johnson" (shooting victim in July 1965 march episode)
pending_context.notes: Speaker confirms a second name in the July 1965 march-shooting episode: "Now it was Milton. Put him in a car with him. Milton ... Milton Johnson ... It was another guy. Milton Johnson's with him. Milton Johnson. That's right. Milton Johnson was getting attacked by the law." Distinct from the principal shooting victim. Speaker-originating; adversarial cross-check against Lance Hill, *The Deacons for Defense* (2004), would canonicalize.

pending_context.whisper: "Jane Brown" (Bogalusa civil rights worker, alongside Helen Jefferson and Canadian Griffin)
pending_context.notes: "There was women like Helen Jefferson. A guy by name was Jane Brown." Speaker's pronoun-and-name pairing is conflicted ("a guy ... Jane Brown"); could be Whisper confusion of a different first name. Adversarial flag.

pending_context.whisper: "AFL from St. Louis" (in re labor-management mediator who came in for police-hiring negotiation)
pending_context.notes: "I had the opportunity to sit down with the negotiating committee that put other, it's abusive from the AFL, from St. Louis, Anna that come in." Speaker is referencing an AFL-CIO regional staff representative ("abusive" → likely "advisor" or "advocate") who came down to Bogalusa for the first-Black-policemen hiring negotiation. Whisper rendering somewhat fragmented.

pending_context.candidate_correction: Praise the Lord, the Disco, Brain Valley Chain" → likely "Praise the Lord, this is co-'
pending_context.notes: Pre-closing benediction by Cynthia or Fletcher in a Black Baptist worship cadence; Whisper failure on a non-standard prayer-phrase. Unresolved — possibly Louisiana French-influenced. Adversarial flag.

pending_context.candidate_correction: the Naval Yard" or "the new yard" — likely a Bogalusa street/intersection
pending_context.notes: "Cookega clan burned across the Nau Yacht" (cross-burning location). Could be a Bogalusa landmark name ("Navy Yard"?), or another rendering of "the yard" referring to Robert Hicks's yard (where the canonical 1965 cross-burning happened). The latter is more likely contextually — speaker is describing the Hicks-yard cross-burning that galvanized the Deacons charter. Adversarial flag.
